title: Notes Receivable — Interest Accrual
event: On October 1, Reyes Co. accepts a $20,000, 6%, nine-month note from a customer. Prepare the December 31 adjusting entry to accrue interest.

lines.label: principal × rate × time
lines.why: Three months (Oct–Dec) of interest have been earned by year-end.
lines.trap: The fraction uses time ELAPSED (3 months), not the note's 9-month term.

lines.label: earned by time
lines.why: Interest is earned by the calendar, not by collection.
lines.trap: The stated 6% is ANNUAL — always prorate by months outstanding over 12.

memorize.body: Interest = Principal × annual rate × time outstanding ÷ 12.
At maturity next year, the collection entry splits interest: 300 to the receivable already accrued, the rest to next year's revenue.

questions.prompt: $20,000 note, 6%, dated Oct 1. Interest accrued at Dec 31 = ?

distractors.feedback: That's a FULL year of interest. Only 3 months have run: 1,200 × 3/12 = 300.
That's the full 9-month TERM. Accrue only the time elapsed by year-end: 3 months = 300.

build.scaffold: Principal × rate × elapsed months ÷ 12. The note's term is a distractor, not an input.
